title: Aluminum Prices Drop as Trump Plans to Roll Back Levies on Metal Products
summary: Aluminum prices fell by up to 2.7% on the London Metal Exchange following a Financial Times report that President Donald Trump intends to reduce levies on metal products, citing consumer harm. The price drop reflects trader reactions to potential tariff reductions on aluminum and steel, with aluminum trading at $3,030 per ton as of 1:03 p.m. local time.

title: J.P. Morgan Forecasts Aluminum Price at $2,200/mt for Q2 2025 Amid Reduced Demand Growth
summary: J.P. Morgan Research predicts an average aluminum price of $2,200 per metric ton in the second quarter of 2025, with global demand growth reduced to 1% year-over-year. This shift is expected to create a surplus of approximately 200,000 metric tons in 2025, while copper prices are forecast to average $8,300/mt in the same period.

title: Aluminum Futures Trade at 3,543.00 with Daily Range Analysis Amid Tariff Rollback Bets
summary: Aluminum futures closed at 3,543.00, up from a previous close of 3,518.48, as traders react to U.S. tariff rollback expectations. Comex aluminum premiums beyond August have declined due to bets on reduced levies, with Reuters reporting price movements in premium contracts on the Comex exchange.

title: Aluminum Price Rises to 3,518.48 USD/T on June 11, 2026, Despite Monthly Decline
summary: Aluminum prices rose to 3,518.48 USD per ton on June 11, 2026, marking a 1.24% increase from the previous day. However, the price has fallen 1.59% over the past month, though it remains 39.53% higher than one year ago, reflecting ongoing market volatility.

title: Aluminum Price Hits Monthly Level of 3,666.10, Up from Previous Month and Year
summary: The monthly aluminum price reached 3,666.10, increasing from 3,599.85 last month and 2,448.79 one year ago. This represents a 1.84% rise from the previous month, highlighting a steady upward trend in aluminum pricing amid global market dynamics.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers reported a nanofiltration process designed to reduce hazardous waste from aluminum production by capturing aluminum ions that would otherwise escape into effluent. In lab-scale tests, the membrane selectively captured more than 99% of aluminum ions, which could both improve yield and reduce waste in manufacturing.

title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop? Latest Hot Rolled Coil ...
summary: Ryerson’s steel market update lists current June 2026 benchmark prices for hot-rolled coil, cold rolled steel, coated steel, and steel plate. It also frames the broader market question of whether prices are beginning to ease.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News

title: Steel Prices Fall Nearly 20% From Their Recent Peak
summary: This video report says steel prices have fallen nearly 20% from a recent peak. It appears to focus on short-term price movement and broader business-market commentary.

title: Global steel industry battles surplus supply, protectionism
summary: The article says steelmaking capacity is expected to keep rising, reaching 2.55 billion metric tons by the end of 2025, with additional planned capacity additions in Asia and the Middle East. It also highlights surplus supply, weak demand in China, and growing trade friction as producers seek new markets.

title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
summary: This piece reviews U.S. steel market conditions, including recent production softness and fluctuating capacity utilization. It notes that short-term indicators showed some stabilization, with mill capability utilization rising to 77.5% in the week of May 10, 2025.

title: Latest developments in steelmaking capacity and outlook until 2026
summary: The report states that global steelmaking capacity remained far above demand, with capacity estimated at 2,432.0 million tonnes in 2023 and excess capacity exceeding global steel exports by a wide margin. It provides an outlook on capacity trends through 2026 and emphasizes the persistence of global overcapacity.

title: Industry Data
summary: AISI reports that U.S. domestic raw steel production for the week ending June 6, 2026 was 1,877,000 net tons, with capability utilization at 81.3%. This provides the latest weekly snapshot of U.S. steel output and operating rates.

title: 3 innovations in green steel
summary: This article highlights several green steel developments, including Boston Metal's CO2-free steel process, a major hydrogen steel plant project in Sweden, and Kobe Steel's lower-coke furnace technique. It emphasizes emerging pathways to reduce emissions in steelmaking through hydrogen, process optimization, and new industrial-scale projects.

title: Top Innovations in Steel Technology to Watch in 2025
summary: This piece surveys major innovation themes in steel, including hydrogen-based production, AI integration, 3D printing, carbon capture, nanotechnology, and recycling. It presents these technologies as key drivers of efficiency, sustainability, and decarbonization in the steel sector.

title: Climate-Tech to Watch: Clean Steel
summary: This report examines pathways for clean steel production and argues that no single technology will fully decarbonize the sector. It identifies energy efficiency, electrification, carbon capture and storage, and low-emissions steelmaking technologies as promising approaches, while noting that many solutions still require additional research and investment.

title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
summary: This article discusses startup-led innovation in iron and steel, organized around reducing demand, improving existing processes, and developing entirely new production methods. It highlights near-zero-emissions steel in Northern Sweden and waste-stream valorization as examples of emerging sustainability-focused approaches.

title: Copper Prices Crash $0.20! Trade Shock Sends Scrappers ...
summary: This video says copper prices dropped by $0.20 per pound after a European trade agreement announcement, creating volatility across the scrap market. It describes traders and yards as confused by the spread swings and rapid move lower.

title: Copper prices hit a new record high, but is the metals trade 'stretched'?
summary: This video reports copper continuing to climb after breaking above $13,000 a ton for the first time. It focuses on whether the metals trade has become overextended after the sharp rally.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics says copper futures rose to around $6.4 per pound, rebounding from three-week lows. The move was tied to rising optimism over a potential US-Iran peace agreement easing market concerns.

title: Copper Prices Pull Back After Strong Rally
summary: iScrap App says copper prices have corrected after reaching multi-year highs. The update frames the move as a pullback after a strong rally in the non-ferrous metals market.

title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
summary: Fastmarkets highlights copper's long-term supply constraints, noting that new supply has been difficult to bring online quickly enough to meet demand. The page emphasizes structural market tightness driven by permitting delays and industry limits.

title: Nickel's New Era: Rising Prices, Growing EV Demand ...
summary: This video discusses a major shift in the nickel market, highlighting price gains from about $14,200 to $16,500 per ton since mid-December 2025. It attributes the move largely to Indonesia’s supply management measures and says further upside toward $18,500-$20,000 is possible if the trend continues.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today
summary: The page shows nickel futures trading at 17,797.38, slightly above the previous close of 17,685.88. It also notes a bearish outlook, citing UBS concerns about a persistent surplus in the global nickel market despite production cuts.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reports nickel around $17,700 per tonne after a sharp decline from above $19,220. The move is framed as profit-taking by investors, which suggests recent volatility and weakening near-term momentum.

title: Nickel prices, news and market analysis
summary: Fastmarkets provides a nickel market news and analysis hub focused on price trends, charts, and volatility. The page is positioned as a source for tracking shifts in the battery raw materials market, including nickel’s role in EV supply chains.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has reportedly told PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine, to sharply reduce output. Bloomberg’s report says the company received a 12 million-ton ore quota for this year, down from 42 million tons in 2025, as Indonesia moves to tighten supply and support prices.

title: Indonesian smelters cut production on lower nickel ore quota
summary: Indonesia’s energy ministry cut the 2026 nickel ore production quota to 260 million to 270 million metric tons, below last year’s 320 million tons of output. The report says the reduction reflects oversupply and weak prices, and it is already forcing smelters to cut production.

title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
summary: This article examines how improving nickel production can support a more sustainable battery industry. It highlights ESG integration, stakeholder engagement, disclosure standards, and process improvements that reduce greenhouse-gas emissions, water use, and energy consumption.

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
summary: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials developed a carbon-free, energy-saving nickel extraction method using hydrogen plasma instead of carbon-based processes. The method reportedly reduces CO2 emissions and energy use while making low-grade nickel ores more viable.

title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
summary: This piece focuses on nickel's expanding role in the clean-energy transition and the need to scale production with less environmental impact. It argues that demand for nickel is rising quickly and that the industry must adopt more sustainable practices.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali to acquire majority stake in its scrap supplier
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali has signed a contract to acquire a 65% controlling interest in Com.Steel Inox, a stainless steel scrap business in Italy. The company said the deal is intended to improve its environmental footprint and optimize operations.

title: Axpo Italia to supply green energy to Tenaris in Italy
summary: Axpo Italia will supply 15 GWh per year of certified green energy to Tenaris's seamless steel pipe mill in Dalmine, Italy. This initiative supports Tenaris's commitment to sustainable practices and reducing carbon emissions in its Italian operations.

title: Tenaris reduced pipe sales to 2.95 million tons in January-September
summary: According to GMK Center, Tenaris's pipe sales decreased by 5% in 2024 compared to 2023, dropping from 4.14 million tons to 3.93 million tons. The report highlights market challenges affecting the company's performance in the first three quarters of the year.

title: Tenaris Temporarily Suspends Operations at certain U.S. Facilities
summary: Tenaris has temporarily suspended operations at its facilities in Koppel, Ambridge (PA), Brookfield (OH), and Baytown (TX). The suspension is part of a strategic adjustment to optimize resource allocation amid changing market conditions.
